title: rubus costatus var. montanus
intro: Rubus costatus var. montanus, also known as the montane bramble, is a variety of the Rubus costatus shrub, a member of the rose family, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus costatus var. montanus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, or other topical applications.
